rationale: In the citing paper, the cited article is not just listed in background; it is included in Table 1 as an analyzed source with many elasticity observations, which directly feeds the meta-analytic results. The text also invokes the cited paper when motivating temporal aggregation effects and when defining promotional price as an index relative to regular price. These are functional uses in model framing and evidence assembly, indicating a meaningful role beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing meta-analysis uses Van Heerde, Leeflang, and Wittink (2000) as one of its underlying empirical sources, extracting multiple elasticity estimates from it for the pooled database. It also cites that study to support key framing points about intertemporal pre/post-promotion sales dynamics and price-index-based price definitions.
